data.title: Sprint drift detection and brainstorm suggestion for relates_to coordination links
data.description:
## Context

Follow-on epic stub. When epics share overlapping problem spaces and are connected via relates_to links, sprint drift detection should follow these links to detect when a related epic's closure may impact in-progress implementation plans. Additionally, brainstorm's shared artifact impact scan should suggest relates_to links when it discovers cross-epic artifact overlap. This epic depends on the shared artifact impact analysis epic (Part C scan) and the Jira bridge LINK sync epic (relates_to infrastructure).

Note: This follow-on epic needs /dso:brainstorm before implementation (depth-capped stub — scrutiny skipped).

## Success Criteria

- Sprint drift detection follows relates_to links between epics: when a related epic transitions to closed after the current epic's implementation plan was created, the drift check flags affected stories for re-planning review via a documented observable contract (REPLAN_TRIGGER event or ticket comment)
- Brainstorm suggests relates_to links when the shared artifact impact scan discovers that artifacts from another open (not closed/archived) epic participate in the current epic's problem space
- Sprint drift false-positive rate is manageable: a coupling-strength signal or threshold distinguishes informational links from structurally-coupled ones

## Dependencies

Shared artifact impact analysis epic (Part C scan provides cross-epic discovery). Jira bridge LINK sync epic (relates_to infrastructure and round-trip sync).

## Approach

Sprint drift detection in sprint-drift-check.sh: one-hop neighbor query (not recursive traversal) on relates_to links, checks whether any related epic closed since the implementation plan timestamp. Brainstorm Part C scan suggests relates_to links to open epics when cross-epic artifact overlap is discovered. False-positive mitigation via coupling-strength signal (TBD during brainstorm).
